title: Union types
instruction:
A union type `A | B` means a value can be either type. Use `typeof` to narrow the type before using it.

Run this.
successMessage: Type narrowing with `typeof` tells TypeScript which branch handles which type — no unsafe casts needed.

title: Literal union types
instruction:
Restrict a value to a specific set of literals. This is safer than a plain string type.

Run this.
successMessage: Literal unions act as exhaustive enumerations. TypeScript will error if you pass a string not in the union.

title: Discriminated unions
instruction:
Add a `kind` (or `type`) discriminant field to each variant so TypeScript can narrow automatically in switch statements.

Run this.
successMessage: Inside each case, TypeScript knows exactly which shape variant you have — no casting needed. This is a core TypeScript pattern.

title: Intersection types
instruction:
An intersection type `A & B` combines both types — the value must satisfy all properties of both.

Run this.
expectedOutput: Hi, I'm Alice and I'm 30 years old.
Hi, I'm Bob and I'm 25 years old.
successMessage: Intersection types are great for composing reusable type pieces without full class inheritance.
